hero.title: Pass your theory test on the first try.
hero.lead: Every question comes with a picture or animation — see the sign, understand the rule, and walk in ready.

tests.heading: Pick the test you're preparing for
tests.sub: Three learning modes for each test — {count} practice questions across Singapore BTT, FTT and RTT.
tests.sub_jp: Three learning modes for each test — {count} practice questions for 普通免許 and 二輪免許.

demo.heading: Try a question right now
demo.sub: No sign-up needed. Tap an answer to see instant feedback — this is how every question works.

how.heading: Learn, practise, then prove it
how.s1_p: Read each rule with the sign or animation beside it, plus a plain-language explanation in your language.
how.s2_p: Answer at your own pace with instant feedback. Every wrong answer shows why, so it sticks.
how.s3_p: Sit a timed exam with the real question count and 90% pass mark. Know you're ready before test day.

centre.heading: Approved driving centres
centre.sub_sg: All Singapore theory tests and training run through three Traffic Police-approved centres.
centre.sub_jp: Find a 公安委員会指定 (government-designated) driving school in your prefecture to start your journey.
centre.jp_1: Japan uses a nationwide system of 公安委員会指定 (government-designated) driving schools. Completing a designated school exempts you from the practical test at the licence centre — you only need to pass the written 本免許学科試験.
centre.jp_2: Choose a school in your prefecture. Most schools offer both manual (MT) and automatic (AT) courses. The full course takes 2–4 months and covers two stages: in-circuit training (第1段階) and on-road training (第2段階).

res.heading: Everything you need to actually learn
res.sub: Free study material that works alongside the practice tests — not just questions, but the understanding behind them.
res.r1_p: Every regulatory, warning and information sign, grouped by type, each with a picture and what to do.
res.r2_p: Speed limits, demerit points, alcohol limits and following distances — shown as simple infographics.
res.r3_p: Every step from registering at a centre to your full licence — for Singapore and for Japan.
res.r4_p: Every traffic term in both your languages, checked by a real person — so a mistranslation never costs you a mark.
res.r5_p: Converting an overseas licence and what Myanmar nationals need to drive in Singapore or Japan.
res.r6_p: Test costs, retake rules, licence validity and the questions new drivers ask most.
res.r7_p: Every class of licence in Singapore and Japan — what vehicle it covers, weight limits and minimum age.
res.r8_p: Full breakdown of theory test fees, lesson costs and total budget for getting your licence.
res.r9_p: Download the Traffic Police handbooks and Japan licence study materials — free for everyone.
res.r10_p: Real tips from premium members who passed their tests — moderated before publishing.
res.r11_p: Class 3/3A final test demerit rules — every mistake, point deduction, and pass threshold explained.

faq.heading: Common questions
faq.sub_sg: Singapore BTT, FTT & RTT — practical answers for learners
faq.sub_jp: Japan 普通免許 & 二輪免許 — practical answers for learners

sg.q: How many questions are in BTT, FTT and RTT — and what's the pass mark?
sg.a: Each Singapore theory test has 50 multiple-choice questions, a 50-minute time limit, and a 90% pass mark (45 correct). Motorcycle riders take RTT instead of FTT after the BTT. TheoryLane mock tests use the same format.

sg.q: What order do I take BTT, FTT and practical lessons?
sg.a: Register at BBDC, CDC or SSDC → pass BTT → start practical lessons → pass FTT (or RTT for motorcycles) after at least 5 car lessons → book the Traffic Police practical test. You cannot skip BTT.

sg.q: Where do I book the theory test and how much does it cost?
sg.a: Book BTT, FTT and RTT on OneMotoring (onemotoring.lta.gov.sg). Each attempt costs about S$7.09 including GST. If you fail, you can rebook after 3 days and pay the fee again.

sg.q: I'm from Myanmar — can I convert my licence or must I take BTT?
sg.a: Myanmar licence holders with a valid Singapore work pass or PR may be able to convert at the Traffic Police — you may not need BTT. If you are on a work permit or have been in Singapore over 12 months without converting, you typically need to pass BTT like other new drivers. Check OneMotoring for your situation.

sg.q: Can I study Singapore theory tests in Myanmar language?
sg.a: Yes. TheoryLane is one of the few BTT/FTT/RTT practice sites with full Myanmar (Burmese) translations for questions and explanations, alongside English — ideal for Myanmar nationals working in Singapore.

jp.q: How many questions are in the Japan final theory test and what's the pass mark?
jp.a: The 本免許学科試験 (final written exam) for car and motorcycle has 95 questions, 50 minutes, and requires 86 correct answers (90%). The provisional 仮免学科 test at the start has 50 questions with the same 90% pass mark.

jp.q: What's the best path for Myanmar nationals getting a Japan licence?
jp.a: Myanmar has no direct licence conversion agreement with Japan. Most people either enrol at a 指定教習所 (driving school) — expensive but reliable — or attempt the 一発試験 at the licence centre, which is cheaper but harder without school training. Either way you must pass the written theory tests.

jp.q: When do I take 仮免許 vs the final 本免許学科試験?
jp.a: At a driving school: pass 仮免学科 + 仮免技能 during Stage 1, get your provisional licence, complete on-road training (Stage 2), graduate from school, then sit only the 本免許学科試験 at the licence centre. The school skill test replaces the centre skill test.

jp.q: Does TheoryLane cover hazard prediction (危険予測) questions?
jp.a: Yes. Japan's car and motorcycle theory exams include hazard-prediction items with ア・イ・ウ sub-questions. TheoryLane includes these question types with illustrations and explanations in Japanese and Myanmar.

jp.q: Can I study Japan theory tests in Myanmar language?
jp.a: Yes. TheoryLane provides Myanmar (Burmese) translations for Japan car (普通免許) and motorcycle (二輪免許) practice questions, alongside Japanese — helpful for Myanmar workers preparing for the 本免許学科試験.

shared.a: Yes. All lesson and practice questions are free. Free accounts get one mock test per category per day. Premium removes ads, unlocks unlimited mock tests, and saves your quiz history.

shared.q: How do I pay for premium and when does it start?
shared.a: Choose monthly or yearly, transfer via KBZPay or WavePay, then submit your transaction ID and payment screenshot. We approve manually — usually within a few hours — and your premium time starts the moment it is confirmed.

pricing.heading: Start free. Go premium when you're serious.
pricing.sub: Pay with KBZPay or WavePay. Premium is confirmed manually, usually within a few hours.

hero.title: Study Guide & Key Facts
hero.lead: Speed limits, alcohol limits, demerit points and stopping distances — the numbers you must know for the test.

sg.title: Singapore

alcohol_limits.title: Alcohol Limits
alcohol_limits.note: Exceeding the limit: fine up to S$10,000 and/or up to 12 months jail for first offence. Disqualification from driving.

demerit_points.title: Demerit Points
demerit_points.note: Common offences: Speeding (+30 km/h over limit = 6–24 points). Running red light = 12 points. Using mobile phone = 6 points.

stopping_distances.title: Stopping Distances (dry road)
stopping_distances.note: Double these distances on wet roads. Always maintain a 2-second following gap in normal conditions.

key_rules: Seat belts are compulsory for ALL occupants — front and rear.
Mobile phone use while driving: illegal unless on hands-free.
Children under 1.35 m must use an approved child restraint.
Minimum following distance: 2 seconds in normal conditions.
Flashing amber at a pedestrian crossing — must yield to pedestrians.
At a stop line with a red light or STOP sign — wheels must stop completely.
Lane discipline: keep left unless overtaking.
Motorcyclists must wear an approved helmet at all times.

jp.title: Japan

alcohol_limits.title: Alcohol Limits (飲酒運転)

items.label: Designated drivers
items.sub: 代行・ハンドルキーパーは飲酒不可

alcohol_limits.note: Passengers who knowingly accompany a drunk driver also face penalties. Providing alcohol to someone about to drive is also illegal.

demerit_points.title: Demerit Points (違反点数)
demerit_points.note: Speeding 30–50 km/h over limit = 6 points. Running a red light = 2 points. Mobile phone while driving = 3 points.

jp.key_rules_title: Key Rules to Know (重要事項)
key_rules: 徐行 (joko) means slow to a speed where you can stop within 1 metre at any time.
At unmarked intersections, yield to vehicles approaching from the right.
Seat belts: compulsory for all seats. Child seats required for children under 6.
Mobile phone use: illegal while driving — even stopping at a red light.
Left turn: cyclists and pedestrians have priority; check mirrors and blind spots.
Horn use: only allowed in emergencies or at designated horn-use zones (警笛鳴らせ).
Headlights must be on in tunnels and at night — even if street lighting is present.
Right of way at intersections: broad road > narrow road; otherwise yield to the right.

hero.title: Licensing Roadmap
hero.lead: Every step from registration to full licence — for Singapore and Japan.

resourcesRoadmap.sg_timeline_body: 4–12 months depending on lesson frequency. Most people take 3–6 months. The theory tests can be taken within 1–2 months of starting.
resourcesRoadmap.jp_title: Japan — 普通自動車免許 (via 指定校)
resourcesRoadmap.jp_timeline_body: 2–4 months for the full course if attending regularly. The minimum course period is set by law and cannot be shortened. Peak seasons (March–April, August) may have longer waiting times.

sg_steps.title: Register at an Approved Centre
sg_steps.body: Choose BBDC (Bukit Batok), CDC (ComfortDelGro), or SSDC (Serangoon). You will need your NRIC/FIN and pass a basic eyesight test. Min. age: 17 (car), 18 (motorcycle).

sg_steps.title: Pass the Basic Theory Test (BTT)
sg_steps.body: 50 multiple-choice questions, 90% pass mark (45/50). Tests traffic signs, road rules and hazard awareness. Book online via OneMotoring. Fee: $7.09 (with GST).

sg_steps.title: Start Practical Lessons
sg_steps.body: Begin driving lessons at the centre. A Class 3 course typically requires 20–35 lessons depending on ability. Each lesson is 1 hour on an in-circuit or road session.

sg_steps.title: Pass the Final Theory Test (FTT)
sg_steps.body: 50 questions on advanced driving — hazard perception, expressway rules, night driving. Must have completed at least 5 practical lessons before booking. Fee: $7.09.

sg_steps.title: Complete Remaining Practical Lessons
sg_steps.body: Continue lessons until the centre certifies you ready. Most centres require a minimum standard before booking the TP (Traffic Police) test.

sg_steps.title: Pass the Traffic Police Driving Test
sg_steps.body: A 45-min driving test with a TP evaluator. Routes are set by the centre. A score of 18 or fewer demerit points passes. Fee: $35.97 (with GST).

sg_steps.title: Collect Your Driving Licence
sg_steps.body: Collect at the Traffic Police Road Safety Community Park or OneMotoring counter. The Probationary Driving Licence (PDL) is displayed on your car for 1 year.

jp_steps.title: 教習所に入学 — Enrol at a Driving School
jp_steps.body: Choose a 公安委員会指定 (government-designated) school in your prefecture. You will need: residence card (住民票), ID photo, and pass an initial health check (eyesight, hearing, etc.).

jp_steps.title: 第1段階 — Stage 1 (In-circuit training)
jp_steps.body: Minimum 10 hours in-circuit driving (manual) or 9 hours (auto). Plus at least 10 hours of theory class covering road rules, signs and safety.

jp_steps.title: 仮免許試験 — Provisional Licence Test
jp_steps.body: A theory test (50 questions, pass mark 90% = 45/50) plus an in-circuit skill test at the school. Passing grants 仮免許 (provisional licence).

jp_steps.title: 第2段階 — Stage 2 (Road training with 仮免)
jp_steps.body: Minimum 19 hours of on-road driving (with instructor) and at least 16 hours of theory class including emergency response and highway driving.

jp_steps.title: 卒業検定 — School Graduation Test
jp_steps.body: A road driving test conducted by the school. Pass and receive a 卒業証明書 (graduation certificate) valid for 1 year. This exempts you from the TP skill test.

jp_steps.title: 本免許学科試験 — Final Theory Test at Licence Centre
jp_steps.body: 95 questions (car) or 95 questions (motorcycle), 90% pass mark (86/95). Tests road signs, traffic rules and case scenarios. Fee: ¥1,750. Book at your prefecture's 免許センター.

jp_steps.title: 免許証交付 — Receive Your Licence
jp_steps.body: After passing the theory test and completing any remaining formalities (vision check, photo) at the licence centre, your 運転免許証 is issued the same day.

hero.title: Costs & Fees
hero.lead: Full breakdown of every test fee, lesson cost, and total budget you need to get your licence in Singapore or Japan.

theory_rows.note: Can only book after ≥ 5 practical lessons.

sg.theory_note: Theory test fees are set by the Traffic Police and do not vary between BBDC, CDC or SSDC.

tp_rows.note: Check current rate at your centre.

sg.lesson_note: Lesson fees increase slightly during peak hours (evenings, weekends). Book off-peak to save. Prices include GST and are approximate for 2025 — check each centre's website for current rates.
sg.total_note: These are estimates only. Registration fees at each centre and admin charges are not included. Check the BBDC, CDC, or SSDC website for the latest lesson packages which may include bundled discounts.

moto_rows.note: Approved helmet required for test and road
Varies significantly by ability and centre

jp.fees_title: Official Test & Licence Fees (免許センター)

fee_rows.item: 仮免許学科試験 (Provisional theory test)
fee_rows.note: At the 免許センター / licence centre

fee_rows.item: 仮免許技能試験 (Provisional skill test)
fee_rows.note: Per attempt. Re-test fee is same.

fee_rows.item: 本免許学科試験 (Final theory test)
本免許技能試験 (Final skill test)
fee_rows.note: Only if going 一発試験 (centre-direct)
Payable at the licence centre on passing

jp.fees_note: Fees are set nationally and do not vary between prefectures. Additional costs (photo, 住民票 certificate) are typically ¥300–¥600 each.
jp.paths_title: Two Paths to a Japanese Licence

path_a.label: Path A
path_a.title: 指定教習所 (Driving School)

rows.label: 本免学科試験 at licence centre

path_a.note: Passing the 卒業検定 exempts you from the 免許センター skill test. You only face the written theory test.

path_b.label: Path B
path_b.title: 一発試験 (Licence Centre Direct)
path_b.note: The skill test at the licence centre is notoriously difficult without school training. Most people need several attempts.

jp.myanmar_intro: Because Myanmar has no reciprocal licence agreement with Japan, you cannot convert directly. You must either attend a driving school or attempt the 一発試験. The recommended path for most people is Path A (指定教習所):

myanmar_rows.label: 本免学科試験 (theory at licence centre)

jp.myanmar_tip: Tip: Some schools offer discount packages for foreign nationals or have staff who speak English. Ask when enrolling. The 本免学科試験 (which this app helps you prepare for) is taken at the end — after completing school.

resourcesCosts.disclaimer_text: All fees are approximate and may change. Singapore fees include GST as of 2025. Japan fees are as published by the 警察庁. Always verify with the relevant authority (Traffic Police / OneMotoring for SG; your prefecture's 免許センター for JP) before budgeting.

hero.title: FAQ & Fees
hero.lead: Official test fees, retake rules, and the questions new drivers ask most.

resourcesFaq.sg_fees_note: Fees are set by the Traffic Police and reviewed periodically. Course lesson fees vary by driving centre and are paid directly to BBDC, CDC, or SSDC.
resourcesFaq.jp_fees_note: Fees at the licence centre are set by prefecture and may vary slightly. 指定自動車教習所 course fees are paid directly to the school.
resourcesFaq.jp_questions_title: よくある質問 — Common Questions

sg_fees.note: Taken after at least 5 practical lessons

jp_fees.note: Written theory test at the licence centre
Practical test at licence centre (指定校 graduates are exempt)
Total cost varies by school and prefecture

sg_faq.q: How many times can I retake the BTT/FTT/RTT?
sg_faq.a: There is no limit on retakes. You pay the fee each time and can rebook after 3 days.

sg_faq.q: Can I book the FTT before completing all practical lessons?
sg_faq.a: You must have completed at least 5 practical lessons (Class 3/3A) before booking the FTT.

sg_faq.q: How long is a Singapore licence valid?
sg_faq.a: A full Class 3/3A licence is valid for 10 years and can be renewed online via OneMotoring.

sg_faq.q: What happens if I accumulate 24 demerit points?
sg_faq.a: Your licence is suspended for 3 months. A new driver (within 1 year of passing) is suspended at 12 points.

sg_faq.q: Is there a minimum age to sit the BTT?
sg_faq.a: Yes — 17 years old for car licence (Class 3/3A) and 18 for motorcycle (Class 2/2A/2B).

sg_faq.q: Which driving centres are Traffic Police approved?
sg_faq.a: Three centres: BBDC (Bukit Batok), CDC (ComfortDelGro), and SSDC (Serangoon). All offer the same standard.

jp_faq.q: 本免許学科試験に落ちたらどうなる？
jp_faq.a: 再受験できます。費用は毎回かかります。次の受験日は翌日以降に予約可能です。

jp_faq.q: 指定校を卒業すると技能試験は免除されますか？
jp_faq.a: はい。公安委員会指定の自動車教習所を卒業すると、免許センターでの技能試験が免除されます。学科試験のみ受験すれば取得できます。
95問中86問以上正解（90%）で合格です（二輪は同様）。

jp_faq.q: 日本の免許証の有効期間は？
jp_faq.a: 取得時の年齢や違反歴によって異なりますが、通常はゴールド免許で5年、一般免許で3年です。
6ヶ月間です。仮免取得後6ヶ月以内に路上教習・卒業試験を修了する必要があります。

hero.title: Bilingual Traffic Glossary
hero.lead: Every key traffic term in English, Myanmar (Burmese) and Japanese — checked by a human so a mistranslation never costs you a mark.

hero.title: Licence Classes
hero.lead: Every class of driving licence in Singapore and Japan — what vehicle it covers, weight limits, minimum age, and key restrictions.

resourcesLicenceClasses.sg_intro: Singapore licences are issued by the Traffic Police. This app covers the theory tests for the three most common classes — Class 3, 3A (cars) and Class 2B (entry motorcycle).
resourcesLicenceClasses.sg_note: Class 3 / 3A requires BTT + FTT + TP practical test. Motorcycle classes (2B/2A/2) require BTT + RTT + TP. GVW = Gross Vehicle Weight.
resourcesLicenceClasses.jp_intro: Japan's licence system is governed by the 道路交通法 (Road Traffic Act). This app covers the 本免許学科試験 for 普通自動車 and 普通二輪 — the two most common paths for Myanmar nationals in Japan.
resourcesLicenceClasses.jp_note: Myanmar nationals cannot directly convert their licence — they must pass the 知識試験 (written test) and 技能試験 (skill test) or complete a Japanese driving school. See our

rows.label: Practical test
values: 指定校卒 or 技能試験
指定校卒 or 技能試験

sg_classes.name: Car — Manual
sg_classes.note: Most common licence. Requires BTT + FTT + TP test.

sg_classes.name: Car — Automatic
sg_classes.note: Restricted to automatic vehicles only. Cannot drive manual cars.

sg_classes.name: Motorcycle — Entry
sg_classes.note: Entry-level motorcycle licence. Requires BTT + RTT + TP test.

sg_classes.name: Motorcycle — Mid
sg_classes.note: Must hold Class 2B for at least 1 year before upgrading.

sg_classes.name: Motorcycle — Full
sg_classes.note: Must hold Class 2A for at least 1 year. Covers all motorcycles.

sg_classes.name: Bus
sg_classes.note: Requires separate bus-specific training and test.

jp_classes.name: Regular Car
jp_classes.vehicle: 普通自動車 — standard car / MPV
jp_classes.note: The standard licence obtained at a 指定教習所. Covers most everyday cars.

jp_classes.name: Semi-medium Truck
jp_classes.vehicle: 準中型自動車 — light-to-medium truck
jp_classes.note: Introduced in 2017. Allows driving delivery trucks from age 18 without waiting.

jp_classes.name: Medium Truck
jp_classes.vehicle: 中型自動車 — medium truck / bus
jp_classes.note: Needed for larger delivery vehicles, minibuses.

jp_classes.name: Large Truck
jp_classes.vehicle: 大型自動車 — large truck / coach
jp_classes.note: Required for heavy goods vehicles, full-size coaches.

jp_classes.name: Regular Motorcycle
jp_classes.vehicle: 普通自動二輪車 — motorcycle / scooter
jp_classes.age: Min. 16 years (小型 ≤125cc) / 18 years (full 普通二輪)
jp_classes.note: 小型限定 (≤125cc) licence is available from age 16. Full licence covers up to 400cc.

jp_classes.name: Large Motorcycle
jp_classes.vehicle: 大型自動二輪車 — large motorcycle
jp_classes.note: Must already hold 普通二輪 (or equivalent). Covers all engine sizes.

jp_classes.name: Moped
jp_classes.vehicle: 原動機付自転車 — moped / scooter
jp_classes.note: Simplest licence — 1-day test at licence centre, no driving school needed.

jp_classes.name: Large Special
jp_classes.vehicle: 大型特殊自動車 — construction machinery
jp_classes.limit: Bulldozers, cranes, forklifts on public road
jp_classes.note: Covers slow-moving construction vehicles on public roads (not off-road operation).

hero.title: Foreigners' Guide
hero.lead: Converting an overseas licence, IDP rules, and what Myanmar nationals need to legally drive in Singapore and Japan.

tourist.title: Using a Foreign Licence as a Tourist
tourist.p1: Holders of a valid foreign driving licence may drive in Singapore for up to 12 months from the date of entry without converting, provided the licence is valid and in English (or accompanied by an English translation).
tourist.p2: An International Driving Permit (IDP) issued in your home country alongside your national licence is also accepted for the same 12-month period.

convert.title: Converting a Foreign Licence (PR / Work Pass Holders)
bullets: Eligible if you hold a valid PR, EP, S Pass, WP, or DP.
Your foreign licence must be valid and issued by a recognised country. Myanmar is on the list — see requirements below.
Visit the Traffic Police (OneMotoring) counter at SLA Building, 8 Shenton Way.
Submit: valid foreign licence + certified English translation (if not in English), FIN card, passport, 1 passport photo.
Pay a fee of approx. S$25–S$50 (check current rates at OneMotoring).
Your Singapore provisional licence is issued; a full licence follows after 1 year with no offences.

myanmar.title: Myanmar Nationals — Specific Requirements 🇲🇲
bullets: Myanmar licence is recognised for conversion — you do not need to sit the Singapore BTT or driving test.
Must have held the Myanmar licence for at least 1 year and have driven on it.
Bring: original Myanmar licence + certified English translation (by a notary or embassy), passport, FIN card.
If your Myanmar licence is not in English, get it translated at the Myanmar Embassy in Singapore or a certified translator.
If you have been in Singapore >12 months without converting, you may need to sit the BTT — check with Traffic Police.

tourist.title: Using a Foreign Licence / IDP in Japan
tourist.p1: Japan accepts an International Driving Permit (IDP) issued under the Geneva Convention 1949. The IDP is valid for 1 year from the date of latest entry into Japan (not from the issue date of the IDP).
tourist.p2: Japan does not accept IDPs issued under the Vienna Convention 1968 — check which convention your home country uses before travelling.

convert.title: Converting a Foreign Licence (外国免許切替)
convert.p1: Japan has reciprocal licence conversion agreements with some countries (e.g. Germany, France, Australia). Myanmar is not on this list, which means:
bullets: You must obtain a certified Japanese translation of your Myanmar licence from JAF (Japan Automobile Federation).
Submit to your prefecture's 免許センター: Myanmar licence + JAF translation, residence card (在留カード), passport, 住民票 (residence certificate), photo.
Take a written knowledge test (10 questions, pass mark 7/10) in Japanese or some centres allow English.
Take a practical skill test (in-circuit) at the licence centre — this tests basic car control and circuit route.
If you pass both, your Japanese licence is issued. JAF translation fee: approx. ¥3,000.

myanmar.title: Myanmar Nationals — Fastest Path 🇲🇲
myanmar.p1: Because Myanmar has no reciprocal agreement with Japan, the fastest legal path to a Japanese licence is:
options: Option A — Driving School: Enrol in a 指定自動車教習所. Expensive (¥250,000–¥400,000) but the school skill test replaces the licence centre skill test. You only need to pass the 本免許学科試験 (theory) at the licence centre. This is the most reliable path.
Option B — Licence Centre Direct: Cheaper, but you must pass the skill test at the licence centre, which is harder to pass first-time without school training.
myanmar.tip: Tip: This app covers the 本免許学科試験 — use the Japan mock tests to prepare.

resourcesForeigners.disclaimer_text: This information is for general guidance only. Requirements may change — always verify with the Traffic Police (Singapore), your prefecture's 免許センター (Japan), or a registered immigration adviser before making decisions.

hero.title: Road Signs Library
hero.lead: Every regulatory, warning and informational sign — with what each one means and what you must do.

signs.title: Stop
signs.subtitle: ရပ်ရမည်
signs.desc: Come to a complete stop at the line before proceeding.

signs.title: Give Way
signs.subtitle: လမ်းပေးရမည်
signs.desc: Yield to traffic on the major road before entering.

signs.title: No Entry
signs.subtitle: ဝင်ခွင့်မပြု
signs.desc: No vehicles may enter this road.

signs.title: Speed Limit 50
signs.subtitle: အမြန်နှုန်း ၅၀
signs.desc: Maximum speed of 50 km/h on this road.

signs.title: No Overtaking
signs.subtitle: ကျော်ဖြတ်ခွင့်မရှိ
signs.desc: Overtaking other vehicles is prohibited ahead.

signs.title: Mandatory Direction
signs.subtitle: ညွှန်ကြားသောဘက် သွားရမည်
signs.desc: Must proceed in the direction shown by the arrow.

signs.title: General Warning
signs.subtitle: သတိပေး
signs.desc: Exercise caution — a hazard lies ahead.

signs.title: Pedestrian Crossing
signs.subtitle: လမ်းဖြတ်ကူးရှိ
signs.desc: Slow down; pedestrians may be crossing ahead.

signs.title: School Zone
signs.subtitle: ကျောင်းဇုန်
signs.desc: Slow to 40 km/h during school hours; children may be present.

signs.title: Traffic Signals Ahead
signs.subtitle: မီးပွိုင့်ရှိ
signs.desc: Traffic lights at the next junction — be prepared to stop.

signs.title: Parking / No Parking
signs.subtitle: ကားရပ်နားခြင်း
signs.desc: Check posted restrictions for times and conditions.

signs.title: Roundabout Ahead
signs.subtitle: လည်ပတ်လမ်းဆုံ
signs.desc: Give way to traffic already in the roundabout.

signs.title: 止まれ
signs.subtitle: Stop
signs.desc: Come to a complete stop at the line. — 停止線の手前で一時停止。

signs.title: 進入禁止
signs.subtitle: No Entry
signs.desc: Vehicles may not enter this road. — この道路へは進入禁止。

signs.title: 最高速度
signs.subtitle: Speed Limit
signs.desc: Do not exceed the posted speed. — 表示速度を超えてはいけない。

signs.title: 追い越し禁止
signs.subtitle: No Overtaking
signs.desc: Overtaking prohibited in this section. — この区間は追い越し禁止。

signs.title: 徐行
signs.subtitle: Proceed Slowly
signs.desc: Slow down and be prepared to stop at any moment. — いつでも止まれる速度で徐行。

signs.title: 一方通行
signs.subtitle: One Way
signs.desc: Traffic flows in one direction only. — 一方向のみ通行可。

signs.title: 駐車禁止
signs.subtitle: No Parking
signs.desc: Stopping to park is prohibited. — 駐車は禁止。

signs.title: 横断歩道
signs.subtitle: Pedestrian Crossing
signs.desc: Yield to pedestrians crossing. — 横断歩道あり、歩行者優先。

signs.title: スクールゾーン
signs.subtitle: School Zone
signs.desc: Slow down; children may be present. — 子供が多い区域、徐行。

signs.title: 警戒
signs.subtitle: Hazard Warning
signs.desc: Danger or unusual road conditions ahead. — 前方に危険箇所あり。

signs.title: 歩行者通行あり
signs.subtitle: Pedestrians Present
signs.desc: Pedestrians may be using this road. — 歩行者の通行に注意。

terms.meta_description: Terms of Service for TheoryLane driving theory test practice.
terms.title: Terms of Service

sections.title: 1. Acceptance
paragraphs: By accessing or using TheoryLane (the "Service"), you agree to these Terms of Service. If you do not agree, please do not use the Service.

sections.title: 2. What we provide
paragraphs: TheoryLane is an independent online practice platform for Singapore (BTT, FTT, RTT) and Japan driving theory tests. We provide lessons, practice questions, mock tests, and study resources in multiple languages.

We are not affiliated with the Singapore Traffic Police, Japan's National Police Agency, any driving centre, or any government licensing authority. Passing our quizzes does not guarantee success on an official exam.

sections.title: 3. Accounts
paragraphs: You may create an account with a valid email address. You are responsible for keeping your login credentials secure and for all activity under your account.
We may suspend or terminate accounts that violate these terms or misuse the Service.

sections.title: 4. Free and Premium
paragraphs: Free users can access limited features, including a daily mock-test limit per category. Premium subscribers receive expanded access for the duration of an active subscription.
Feature limits may change; we will try to give reasonable notice of material changes.

sections.title: 5. Payments
paragraphs: Premium may be purchased via manual payment methods shown in the app (e.g. KBZPay, WavePay). Payment screenshots are reviewed manually; access is granted after approval.
Prices and subscription duration are shown at checkout. Refunds are handled case by case — contact us if you believe a payment was made in error.

sections.title: 6. Acceptable use
paragraphs: You agree not to copy, scrape, or redistribute our question banks or content; attempt to bypass paywalls or rate limits; use bots or automated tools to abuse the Service; or upload harmful, illegal, or misleading content in feedback forms.
We may block access for abuse without prior notice.

sections.title: 7. Intellectual property
paragraphs: All questions, illustrations, branding, and software on TheoryLane are owned by us or our licensors. You receive a personal, non-transferable licence to use the Service for your own study only.

sections.title: 8. Disclaimers
paragraphs: The Service is provided "as is" for educational purposes. We do not warrant that content is complete, current, or error-free. Always refer to official handbooks and licensing authorities for exam rules.

sections.title: 9. Limitation of liability
paragraphs: To the fullest extent permitted by law, TheoryLane and its operators are not liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages arising from your use of the Service, including exam outcomes or lost data.

sections.title: 10. Changes
paragraphs: We may update these Terms from time to time. Continued use after changes are posted constitutes acceptance of the updated Terms.

sections.title: 11. Governing law
paragraphs: These Terms are governed by the laws of Singapore, without regard to conflict-of-law principles. Disputes should first be raised with us by email.

about.meta_description: About TheoryLane — Singapore and Japan driving theory test practice in English, Myanmar, and Japanese.
about.title: About TheoryLane

sections.title: Our mission
paragraphs: TheoryLane helps migrant workers and learners prepare for Singapore and Japan driving theory tests in a language they understand — English, Myanmar (Unicode), or Japanese.
We built the app because official materials are often hard to access abroad, and many learners need bilingual explanations alongside realistic practice.

sections.title: What you can do here
paragraphs: Study by topic with lesson mode, drill weak areas in practice mode, and sit timed mock tests that mirror real exam formats.
Singapore: BTT, FTT, and RTT. Japan: car and motorcycle theory. Resources include sign guides, handbooks, FAQs, and cost breakdowns.

sections.title: Independent study tool
paragraphs: TheoryLane is not affiliated with the Singapore Traffic Police, Japan's National Police Agency, CDCs, or any driving school. All questions and illustrations are original study material — not copied from official exams.
Always confirm rules, fees, and booking steps with the relevant authority or driving centre.

sections.title: Premium & support
paragraphs: A free tier lets you try the platform daily. Premium unlocks unlimited mock tests and removes ads.
For payment help, bugs, or content feedback, email us or use the Report issue form — we read every message.

footer.tagline: Singapore & Japan driving theory test practice — in your language.
footer.report_title: Found a bug or something not working?
footer.report_desc: Tell us what went wrong — we read every report and fix issues quickly.
footer.note: An independent study tool. Not affiliated with the Singapore Traffic Police, Japan's National Police Agency, or any driving centre. All questions and illustrations are original.

feedback.title: Report a problem or send feedback
feedback.lead: Found a bug, something confusing, or have a suggestion? Tell us what happened and we'll look into it.
feedback.body_placeholder: What were you doing? What did you expect? What happened instead?
feedback.email_hint: Required if you're not signed in — we use it only to follow up on your report.
feedback.success_title: Thank you — we received your report.
feedback.success_body: Our team will review it. If you're signed in, you can track status below.
feedback.error_generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.
feedback.no_reports: You haven't submitted any reports yet.
feedback.signin_hint: Sign in to track your reports and skip entering email each time.

hero.title: Download official handbooks
hero.lead: Free links to government handbooks and study guides for Singapore and Japan theory tests.

resourcesHandbook.disclaimer: These files are hosted by the official authorities. TheoryLane is an independent study tool and is not affiliated with any government agency.

hero.title: Tips from members who passed
hero.lead: Premium members share real study tips and test-day experiences. Every review is checked by our team before it goes live.

experiences.signin_prompt: Sign in to share your experience with other learners.
experiences.premium_prompt: Only premium members can submit reviews. Upgrade to share your story.
experiences.write_prompt: Passed your test or want to help others? Share your experience.
experiences.approval_note: Your review will appear after admin approval — usually within 24 hours.
experiences.submit_success: Thanks! Your review is pending approval.
experiences.empty: No published reviews yet. Be the first premium member to share your story!

practicalChecklist.sg_only_lead: Switch your country to Singapore in the header to view the Class 3 / 3A demerit scoring guide.
practicalChecklist.jp_only_lead: This demerit reference is for Singapore Class 3/3A only. For Japan, see the licence roadmap and study guide for 技能試験 (practical) steps after driving school.
practicalChecklist.pass_demerit_detail: Your total demerits must stay below {max}. At {max} or more, you fail.
practicalChecklist.pass_immediate_detail: Circled items on the form (e.g. red light, accident) end the test — regardless of your demerit total.
practicalChecklist.pass_items_detail: Official Traffic Police form — each row below shows the deduction for that mistake.
practicalChecklist.legend_box: Box on form = demerit points shown
practicalChecklist.disclaimer: Based on the Traffic Police Class 3/3A final assessment form. For study reference only — not an official test record.
practicalChecklist.critical_title: Critical moments — read before test day
practicalChecklist.critical_sub: Step-by-step procedures and warnings for the situations that fail candidates most often.
practicalChecklist.sections_intro: Each item lists the demerit deducted when the examiner marks that mistake on the official form.

site.title: Singapore Driving Theory Test — BTT, FTT, RTT Practice | TheoryLane
site.description: Free Singapore driving theory test practice for BTT, FTT and RTT. 1,000+ questions in English and Myanmar. Mock exams with real pass marks.

home.title: Singapore Driving Theory Test — BTT, FTT, RTT Practice | TheoryLane
home.description: Pass your Singapore theory test on the first try. Free BTT, FTT and RTT practice questions, mock tests, road signs and study guides — in English and Myanmar.

quiz.description: Free {test} practice with {count}+ questions. Timed mock exam: {mock} questions, {pass}% pass mark. Study in English or Myanmar on TheoryLane.

btt.meta_title: BTT Practice Test Singapore — Free Basic Theory Test | TheoryLane
btt.meta_description: Free Singapore BTT (Basic Theory Test) practice with 565+ questions. Mock exam: 50 questions, 90% pass mark. Learn traffic signs and road rules in English or Myanmar.
btt.h1: BTT Practice Test — Singapore Basic Theory Test
btt.lead: Prepare for your Singapore Basic Theory Test (BTT) with free lesson, practice and mock exam modes. Every question includes a sign or illustration plus explanations in English and Myanmar.
btt.about_p1: The Basic Theory Test (BTT) is the first theory exam every new driver in Singapore must pass. It covers traffic signs, road markings, traffic rules and basic hazard awareness before you can start practical driving lessons at BBDC, CDC or SSDC.
btt.about_p2: The official exam has 50 multiple-choice questions with a 90% pass mark (45 correct). TheoryLane mirrors this format so you know you are ready before booking on OneMotoring.
format_items: Book via OneMotoring — fee approx. S$7.09
btt.modes_learn: Lesson — read each rule with the sign beside it
btt.modes_practice: Practice — answer at your own pace with instant feedback
btt.modes_mock: Mock test — timed 50-question exam, real pass mark

faq.q: How many questions are in the Singapore BTT?
faq.a: The official Basic Theory Test has 50 multiple-choice questions. You need 45 correct (90%) to pass.

faq.q: Is BTT practice free on TheoryLane?
faq.a: Yes. All BTT lesson and practice questions are free. Free accounts get one mock test per day; premium unlocks unlimited mock tests.

faq.q: Can I study BTT in Myanmar language?
faq.a: Yes. TheoryLane is one of the few BTT practice sites with full Myanmar (Burmese) translations for questions and explanations.

faq.q: When can I take the BTT?
faq.a: You can book the BTT after registering at an approved driving centre. Minimum age is 17 for car (Class 3/3A) and 18 for motorcycle.

btt.stats: {count}+ practice questions · {mock} mock exam · {pass}% pass mark

ftt.meta_title: FTT Practice Test Singapore — Final Theory Test Mock Exam | TheoryLane
ftt.meta_description: Free Singapore FTT (Final Theory Test) practice with 516+ questions. Timed mock exam: 50 questions, 90% pass mark. Hazard perception and advanced driving rules.
ftt.h1: FTT Practice Test — Singapore Final Theory Test
ftt.lead: Master the Final Theory Test (FTT) after your practical lessons. Practice hazard perception, expressway rules and advanced driving scenarios — free, with Myanmar language support.
ftt.about_p1: The Final Theory Test (FTT) is taken after you have completed at least five practical driving lessons. It focuses on hazard perception, expressway driving, night driving and more advanced scenarios than the BTT.
ftt.about_p2: Like the BTT, the FTT has 50 questions, a 50-minute time limit and a 90% pass mark. Passing the FTT is required before you can book your Traffic Police practical driving test.
ftt.modes_learn: Lesson — study advanced rules with visuals
ftt.modes_mock: Mock test — full timed FTT simulation

faq.q: What is the difference between BTT and FTT?
faq.a: BTT covers basic signs and rules — taken before practical lessons. FTT covers advanced topics like hazard perception and expressways — taken after at least five practical lessons.

faq.q: How many FTT practice questions does TheoryLane have?
faq.a: TheoryLane includes 516+ FTT practice questions across lesson, practice and mock test modes.

faq.q: Can I retake the FTT if I fail?
faq.a: Yes. There is no limit on retakes, but you pay the fee each time. You can rebook after three days.

faq.q: Do I need to pass BTT before FTT?
faq.a: Yes. You must pass the BTT first, complete practical lessons, then pass the FTT before booking the Traffic Police driving test.

ftt.stats: {count}+ practice questions · {mock} mock exam · {pass}% pass mark

rtt.meta_title: RTT Practice Test Singapore — Riding Theory Test | TheoryLane
rtt.meta_description: Free Singapore RTT (Riding Theory Test) practice for motorcycle licence Class 2B, 2A and 2. 109+ questions, mock exam with 90% pass mark. English and Myanmar.
rtt.h1: RTT Practice Test — Singapore Riding Theory Test
rtt.lead: Preparing for a motorcycle licence? Practice the Riding Theory Test (RTT) for Class 2B, 2A and 2 with free questions, explanations and timed mock exams.
rtt.about_p1: The Riding Theory Test (RTT) replaces the FTT for motorcycle licence classes (2B, 2A, 2). You still take the BTT first, then the RTT instead of the FTT before your Traffic Police riding test.
rtt.about_p2: The RTT has the same format as BTT and FTT: 50 questions, 50 minutes and a 90% pass mark, with content focused on motorcycle-specific rules and hazards.
format_items: Taken instead of FTT for motorcycle riders

faq.q: Do motorcycle riders take FTT or RTT?
faq.a: Motorcycle riders take the RTT (Riding Theory Test), not the FTT. You still need to pass the BTT first.

faq.q: What licence classes does RTT cover?
faq.a: RTT applies to Class 2B (≤200cc), Class 2A (201–400cc) and Class 2 (unlimited) motorcycle licences.
Yes. TheoryLane offers free RTT lesson and practice modes, plus one mock test per day on the free plan.

faq.q: What is the RTT pass mark?
faq.a: You need 45 out of 50 correct answers (90%) to pass the RTT, the same as BTT and FTT.

rtt.stats: {count}+ practice questions · {mock} mock exam · {pass}% pass mark
